assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items discuss heightened Strait of Hormuz risk (e.g., CNBC framing an economic 'deadline'), but none show a named major container carrier (Maersk/MSC/CMA CGM/Hapag-Lloyd/ONE) publicly announcing a 7+ day suspension/avoidance of Hormuz transits.
No bullet references a nationwide Iran internet outage or confirmation from monitoring groups such as NetBlocks, Cloudflare Radar, Kentik, or IODA.
While gas prices and Iran-war-related oil risk are featured, there is no cited announcement of an IEA coordinated release (≥60M bbl) or a U.S. SPR release/sale (≥30M bbl).

Market coverage notes volatility and regional equity drops (e.g., Nikkei/Kospi), but none provides the Russell 2000 close relative to its 2026-03-20 level or confirms a ≥20% drawdown threshold breach.
The OpenAI-linked CNBC piece is about data-center strategy and Wall Street concerns, not a posted list-price reduction of a flagship text-generation API by ≥30% versus 2026-03-22.
No bullet indicates a new ClinicalTrials.gov registration for a Phase 3 randomized oral LDL-lowering therapy trial with planned enrollment ≥2,000.
No bullet references a CDC MMWR or HAN advisory mentioning fentanyl distributed on paper/blotter-like carriers (e.g., paper strips/blotter paper).
No bullet reports the DoD publishing revised Pentagon press-access policy/guidance that explicitly references compliance with a 2026 court order/ruling.

Despite extensive coverage of conflict-driven fuel-price pressure, there is no cited SEC filing or earnings-call transcript from Delta/United/American/Southwest/JetBlue/Alaska stating increased jet-fuel hedging or added/expanded fuel surcharges versus the prior quarter due to Middle East conflict risk.
